oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Ringing your own cowbell: How to engage in organized vet med
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Brock, Elizabeth
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: career
leadership
volunteerism
service
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Organized veterinary medicine plays a critical role in shaping the future of the profession, supporting individual veterinar┬¡ians and advocating for animal and public health. Active in┬¡volvement in organized veterinary medicine by veterinarians early in their careers ÔÇô through professional associations, stu┬¡dent organizations and specialty groups ÔÇô offers benefits that extend from personal career development to sustainability in practice. These benefits include professional networking, con┬¡tinuing education, policy advocacy, mentorship, leadership development and the advancement of public trust in the veteri┬¡nary field. In an increasingly consolidated and isolated busi┬¡ness landscape, veterinarians who engage with these organiza┬¡tions not only advance their own careers, but also contribute to the sustainability and integrity of the profession.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 172-174
